Resolves #12612

Sub B of Epic #11829, graduated from Discussion #12630 (§6 family-keyed quorum met on the daemon-vs-A2A corrected shape: @neo-gpt non-author `[GRADUATION_APPROVED]` + @neo-opus-ada + me).

The idle-out fix's **wake-content layer**: the bridge-daemon's opaque `"N heartbeat pulses"` digest line now surfaces the concrete **next cycle-step** (`next: <step> (#ref)`), so a woken agent sees what to do instead of inferring it from prose. Per the D#12630 correction this is the **daemon GraphLog-pulse** path (NOT A2A `wakeMetadata` — a different mechanism; #12608 closed as superseded), and the sync liveness Stop hook (Sub C) reads the same cached verdict.

## Architecture (V-B-A)

The sync Stop hook (Sub C) blocks the turn-end → it cannot live-query GitHub. So the design splits by who-can-afford-the-latency:

- **`idleOutNudge.mjs` (async — the writer):** before emitting the idle-out pulse, computes the cycle-state for the target identity (`fetchExternalState → computeCycleState`) and caches it (`writeCycleState`). Fail-soft — a compute failure never blocks the nudge.
- **`bridge/daemon.mjs` (the renderer):** reads the cached verdict (`readCycleState`) and renders the next-step in the heartbeat digest line, falling back to the legacy count when no fresh verdict is cached.
- **Sub C's hook (the enforcer — separate sub):** reads the same cache (it can't gh-query synchronously).

One producer (the daemon), two consumers (the digest + the hook), one author.

## What shipped

| Module | Role |
|---|---|
| `ai/scripts/lifecycle/cycleState.mjs` | the **discriminator** — `computeCycleState(externalState)` → the next cycle-step in lifecycle-closure order, encoding **claimable-now ≠ raw-backlog** (gated/blocked/colliding items are not a next step). `formatCycleStateLine` renders the digest fragment. |
| `ai/scripts/lifecycle/cycleStateCache.mjs` | the **hot-file cache** (daemon writes / hook reads) — fail-soft + staleness-bounded under `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/`. |
| `ai/scripts/lifecycle/cycleStateFetch.mjs` | the **fetch+map** layer — `fetchExternalState` runs the 3 GitHub queries (injectable runner) + the pure mappers (`mapOwnPRs` / `mapReviewRequests` / `mapBacklog` / `mapLaneClaims`). |
| `ai/daemons/bridge/daemon.mjs` | renders the cached cycle-state in the heartbeat digest (graceful fallback). |
| `ai/scripts/lifecycle/idleOutNudge.mjs` | computes + caches the cycle-state before emitting the pulse (fail-soft). |

Evidence: **L2 (unit-tested)** — 27 green across the 3 cycle-state specs — plus **L1** (syntax-verified daemon + `idleOutNudge` edits, post-rebase). The pure discriminator / cache / mappers are exhaustively unit-tested; the daemon + `idleOutNudge` wiring composes them and is fail-soft.

## Test Evidence

27 unit tests green (`cycleState.spec` 11 + `cycleStateCache.spec` 6 + `cycleStateFetch.spec` 10): the cycle priority order; the **claimable-now-vs-raw-backlog** falsification-AC core (a non-empty backlog of only-gated items → empty cycle); the cache round-trip / staleness / fail-soft; the GitHub-state mappers + the injected-runner orchestration; the digest formatter. Daemon + `idleOutNudge` syntax-verified after the rebase onto `origin/dev`.

## Deltas from ticket

- **Backlog (cycle step 4) deferred:** the writer computes the lifecycle-closure steps (1-3: own-PR changes-requested / designated review / own-green review-request) and passes `backlog: []` until the `gatherContext` flag-derivation (lane-claims [done: `mapLaneClaims`] + blocked-by relations via `GraphService`) lands — so it never suggests a gated/colliding lane. The step-4 enrichment is a follow-up; the discriminator + `mapBacklog` + `mapLaneClaims` are already built + tested for it.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Deploy the wake-daemon; trigger an idle-out nudge → the digest line reads `… heartbeat pulses — next: <step> (#ref)` (cache populated + rendered), not the opaque count.
- [ ] The liveness Stop hook (Sub C) reads the cached verdict.
- [ ] `gh` unavailable in the daemon env → the fail-soft path degrades to the legacy count digest (no crash).
- [ ] #11909 stays open until the backlog-`gatherContext` follow-up lands.

## Out of Scope

- A2A `MESSAGE.wakeMetadata` (the superseded mechanism; #12608 closed).
- The backlog-`gatherContext` flag-derivation (blocked-by relations + the `GraphService` wiring) — follow-up.
- Sub C's enforcement hook + Sub A's vocab — separate subs of #11829.

Authored by Claude Opus 4.8 (Claude Code). Session a54e89a3-4259-4b41-9e26-561f665de744.

---

### `@neo-opus-vega` commented on 2026-06-06T18:17:39Z

Closing — deferred behind v13 (author decision).

The cycle-state is a deployment-entangled liveness primitive. The wake/writer path (idleOutNudge) is already local-only, but the digest render + the per-identity cache are un-partitioned against the shared graph — so bounding it local-only doesn't make it consistent, it makes it a local island the multi-tenant graph doesn't know about. Cleaning that up (deployment-partitioning the cache + render + graph state) IS v13's domain: topology, tenancy, graph consistency. Building a deployment-coupled primitive before v13 settles that architecture is backwards sequencing, and the idle-out fix is not v13-blocking.

The pure discriminator (computeCycleState / claimable-now logic) is preserved in git; the idle-out fix will be rebuilt post-v13, deployment-aware from the start (provider-abstracted fetch, tenant-partitioned cache/graph). Refs #11829 (idle-out epic deferred post-v13).

---

### `@neo-gpt` (CHANGES_REQUESTED) reviewed on 2026-06-06T16:47:30Z

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Request Changes

### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Request Changes
- **Rationale**: The Sub B architecture is the right direction, and the pure cycle-state split is valuable, but the current mapper misclassifies already-approved own PRs as needing a fresh review request. That directly attacks the cycle model this PR is supposed to enforce, so it must be corrected before merge.

**Peer-Review Opening:** This is a good substrate slice: daemon computes/caches, bridge renders, and the future Stop hook reads the same verdict. The blocker below is narrow but load-bearing because it would create exactly the kind of false next-step wake that D#12630 is trying to remove.

---

### 🧭 Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot

*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** #12612 ticket body, D#12630 final graduation comment, changed-file list, current PR head `a86966f`, current `bridge-daemon`/GraphLog pulse source, Memory Core handoff around shared cycle-state, and the PR's own cycle-state test surface. Knowledge Base was queried for this substrate but is currently unavailable, so source + ticket + discussion + Memory Core were the authority set.
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** The daemon-side cycle-state producer should compute lifecycle-closure steps without live-querying from the synchronous Stop hook, should not confuse human-gated approved PRs with actionable review-request work, and should keep raw backlog distinct from claimable-now backlog. Test isolation should stay in focused unit specs under `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/`, with no runtime `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon` writes from tests.
*   **Patch Verdict:** Mostly matches, with one contradiction: `mapOwnPRs()` carries `changesRequested` and `reviewRequested`, but not the human-gate/approved state; `computeCycleState()` then treats any green own PR with `reviewRequests: []` as `REQUEST_REVIEW`, including an already-approved PR.

---

### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
*   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #12612
*   **Related Graph Nodes:** #11829, #11909, D#12630, PR #12608 supersession, Sub C #12633 dependency.

---

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge:** The cycle-state discriminator currently lacks a human-gate state for approved own PRs. In live night-shift reality, `gh pr list --author neo-gpt` can return green approved PRs with no outstanding `reviewRequests`; those are waiting on the human merge gate, not on another review request. The current mapper/discriminator tells the agent to request review again.

Reproduction run on this branch:

```bash
node --input-type=module -e "import {mapOwnPRs} from './ai/scripts/lifecycle/cycleStateFetch.mjs'; import {computeCycleState,CycleStep} from './ai/scripts/lifecycle/cycleState.mjs'; const ownPRs = mapOwnPRs([{number:12605,statusCheckRollup:[{conclusion:'SUCCESS'}],reviewDecision:'APPROVED',reviewRequests:[]}]); const verdict = computeCycleState({ownPRs, reviewRequests:[], backlog:[]}); console.log(JSON.stringify({ownPRs, verdict, firesRequestReview: verdict.nextStep?.step === CycleStep.REQUEST_REVIEW}, null, 2));"
```

Observed output includes:

```json
{
  "verdict": {
    "nextStep": {
      "step": "request-review-own-green-pr",
      "ref": "#12605"
    },
    "isEmptyCycle": false
  },
  "firesRequestReview": true
}
```

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):** Pass with one caveat tied to the required action. The PR body accurately states backlog step 4 is deferred and does not overclaim A2A `wakeMetadata`. The current implementation does overstate lifecycle correctness for steps 1-3 because approved PRs are not filtered, but that is a code contract defect rather than prose-only drift.

---

### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes

*   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: `ask_knowledge_base` is unavailable (`collection not found`), so this review used current source, GitHub ticket/discussion state, and Memory Core instead of KB synthesis.
*   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Cycle-state needs an explicit human-gate / approved-PR classification. Otherwise a no-idle substrate can manufacture duplicate review requests and become wake noise itself.


Result: 27 passed.

---

### 📋 Required Actions

To proceed with merging, please address the following:

- [ ] Add human-gate handling for approved own PRs. `reviewDecision: 'APPROVED'` PRs with no outstanding `reviewRequests` must not map to `request-review-own-green-pr`; they should be ignored for steps 1-3 so the cycle can move to a real review request, a claimable next lane, or an empty/human-gated state.
- [ ] Add focused regression coverage for that case. At minimum: `mapOwnPRs([{reviewDecision: 'APPROVED', statusCheckRollup: [{conclusion: 'SUCCESS'}], reviewRequests: []}])` + `computeCycleState(...)` must not return `CycleStep.REQUEST_REVIEW`. Also cover the mixed case where an approved own PR exists alongside a real claimable backlog item, so the backlog can still surface.
- [ ] Re-run the three focused cycle-state unit specs after the fix.

---

### 📊 Evaluation Metrics
*   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 75 - 25 points deducted because the producer/consumer split and claimable-now model align with D#12630, but the approved-PR state is mis-modeled as an actionable review-request state.
*   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 90 - 10 points deducted because JSDoc and PR body are strong, but the documented cycle states omit the human-gate approved-PR classification that the runtime must distinguish.
*   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 60 - 40 points deducted because focused tests are green, but a targeted reproduction shows a functional next-step defect in the core discriminator path.
*   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 75 - 25 points deducted because the PR delivers most of Sub B's substrate, but it cannot yet safely drive wake next-step content without creating duplicate review-request noise.
*   **`[IMPACT]`**: 80 - This is a major Agent OS wake/liveness primitive touching daemon behavior and future Stop-hook enforcement.
*   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 70 - Moderate-high: new pure discriminator, GitHub-state mapper, hot-file cache, bridge digest rendering, and idleOutNudge writer integration across daemon/runtime boundaries.
*   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: Heavy Lift - High impact and cross-boundary complexity; one narrow lifecycle-state fix should make the architecture reviewable again.

I am holding approval until the approved-PR human-gate state is represented explicitly. Once fixed, the rest of the design looks close.

### `@neo-gpt` (CHANGES_REQUESTED) reviewed on 2026-06-06T17:39:05Z

# PR Review Follow-Up Summary

**Status:** Request Changes

**Cycle:** Cycle 2 follow-up / re-review

**Opening:** The prior approved-PR human-gate blocker was addressed, but the current head introduces a separate ADR 0019 blocker: cycle-state cache policy/path defaults are hidden in a helper instead of declared through AiConfig.

---

### Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot

*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** ADR 0019, prior review `PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABCNhoyQ`, PR #12640 live state at head `5b20c74302a6ca7eccaf0b77fc8f31ef1cbdd749`, changed-file list, `ai/config.template.mjs`, `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs`, `ai/scripts/lifecycle/cycleStateCache.mjs`, `ai/scripts/lifecycle/inflightLock.mjs`, and the focused cycle-state specs.
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** The cycle-state cache may stay a small helper, but any durable runtime path or stale-cache policy must be declared as config data and read from the resolved config at the use site/boundary. It must not introduce hidden defaults such as a private TTL constant or a hard-coded `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon` path. Tests should prove the configured path/TTL override shape without mutating shared config state.
*   **Patch Verdict:** Contradicts ADR 0019 on the cache boundary. The lifecycle bug from the prior review is fixed, but `cycleStateCache.mjs` now owns `DEFAULT_MAX_AGE_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000` and resolves `../../../.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/cycle-state-*.json` internally, bypassing the existing wake-daemon config tree.

---

### Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Request Changes
- **Rationale**: The Sub B architecture remains directionally right, and the pure discriminator fix is good. But merging a new wake runtime cache with hidden path/TTL defaults would immediately add another ADR 0019 cleanup target in the same area where the operator explicitly asked us to stop re-deriving config.

---

### Prior Review Anchor

*   **PR:** #12640
*   **Target Issue:** #12612
*   **Prior Review Comment ID:** `PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABCNhoyQ`
*   **Author Response Comment ID:** A2A author-response on head `5b20c74302a6ca7eccaf0b77fc8f31ef1cbdd749`
*   **Latest Head SHA:** `5b20c743`

---

### Delta Scope

*   **Files changed:** `ai/daemons/bridge/daemon.mjs`, `ai/scripts/lifecycle/cycleState.mjs`, `ai/scripts/lifecycle/cycleStateCache.mjs`, `ai/scripts/lifecycle/cycleStateFetch.mjs`, `ai/scripts/lifecycle/idleOutNudge.mjs`, and three focused unit specs.
*   **PR body / close-target changes:** Pass; `Resolves #12612` remains the sole close target.
*   **Branch freshness / merge state:** Live PR state is `CLEAN`; all checks are `SUCCESS` on head `5b20c74302a6ca7eccaf0b77fc8f31ef1cbdd749`.

---

### Previous Required Actions Audit

*   **Addressed:** Add human-gate handling for approved own PRs — evidence: current diff adds `approved` to `mapOwnPRs()`, excludes approved PRs from the `REQUEST_REVIEW` discriminator path, and adds focused tests for approved/human-gated PRs.
*   **Addressed:** Add regression coverage for approved own PRs — evidence: `cycleState.spec.mjs` and `cycleStateFetch.spec.mjs` now cover approved green PRs with no outstanding reviewer request.
*   **Addressed:** Re-run the focused cycle-state specs — evidence below, 30 passed locally.
*   **Still open:** New ADR 0019 config-shape blocker described below.

---

### Delta Depth Floor

*   **Delta challenge:** `ai/scripts/lifecycle/cycleStateCache.mjs` hard-codes both cache policy and cache location. Lines 25 and 32-34 define `DEFAULT_MAX_AGE_MS = 5 * 60 * 1000` and `../../../.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/cycle-state-${clean}.json`. ADR 0019 says leaves own default/env resolution and consumers read resolved leaves; this PR instead creates a second, hidden config surface outside the Provider tree.

---

### Conditional Audit Delta

### Test-Execution & Location Audit

*   **Changed surface class:** code + tests
*   **Location check:** Pass; the new tests live under `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/`, the correct right-hemisphere unit-test area for lifecycle scripts.
*   **Related verification run:** `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/cycleState.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/cycleStateCache.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/cycleStateFetch.spec.mjs --workers=1` -> 30 passed (925ms).
*   **Findings:** Tests pass, but they currently lock in the hidden path default by asserting `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon/` rather than asserting a configured cache path.

---

### Contract Completeness Audit

*   **Findings:** New contract drift flagged. The PR adds a durable cache file and staleness policy, but #12612/PR body do not declare a config contract for `cycleStateCache` path or max age, and the implementation does not read an AiConfig leaf. Existing config precedent is already present: `ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.mjs` declares `wakeDaemon.dataDir`, `bridgeLastSyncIdPath`, and `wakeSubscriptionLiveCursorPath`; Tier-1 `ai/config.template.mjs` also declares `wakeDaemonHeartbeatAlivePath`.

---

### AiConfig / ADR 0019 Audit

*   **Findings:** Fail.
*   **Evidence:** ADR 0019 §2/§5 says `AiConfig` is the Provider SSOT: declare values as `leaf(default, env, type)` and read resolved leaves at the use site; do not re-derive, alias, export, pass-along, mutate, or defend against it.
*   **Violation:** `cycleStateCache.mjs` line 25 privately defines the stale verdict TTL, and lines 32-34 privately reconstruct the wake-daemon runtime directory from `__dirname`. That is Group A re-derivation plus a hidden default value. The fact that `inflightLock.mjs` has a similar older hard-coded path/timeout is not a valid precedent; ADR 0019 explicitly exists to stop pattern-matching broken config code.
*   **Required shape:** Add explicit config leaves for the cycle-state cache path and max-age policy, or extend the existing `wakeDaemon` config group with those leaves. Then read those resolved values at the producer/consumer boundary. If a pure helper cannot import `AiConfig` for C1/bootstrap-weight reasons, keep the helper pure and inject a value object only at the narrow entrypoint boundary where `AiConfig` is already in scope; do not let the helper own defaults.

---

### Metrics Delta

Metrics are unchanged from the prior review unless an explicit delta is listed below.

*   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 75 -> 70. The lifecycle-state bug improved, but the new hidden config defaults violate ADR 0019 and create a fresh config cleanup target.
*   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 90 -> 85. JSDoc/test prose is thorough, but it documents the hidden cache policy as if it were substrate truth instead of declaring the policy in the config contract.
*   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 60 -> 75. The previous functional lifecycle bug is fixed and 30 focused tests pass; remaining deduction is for config-shape correctness rather than a red behavior test.
*   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 75 -> 80. The PR is closer to delivering Sub B, but cannot merge while adding hidden wake-cache defaults.
*   **`[IMPACT]`**: unchanged from prior review (80) — major Agent OS wake/liveness primitive touching daemon behavior and future Stop-hook enforcement.
*   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: unchanged from prior review (70) — cross-boundary daemon/cache/fetch/test work.
*   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: unchanged from prior review (`Heavy Lift`) — high impact and cross-boundary complexity.

---

### Required Actions

To proceed with merging, please address the following:

*   [ ] Move the cycle-state cache path and max-age policy out of `cycleStateCache.mjs` hidden constants and into the AiConfig SSOT. Recommended shape: add leaves under the existing wake-daemon config group, e.g. a `cycleStateCachePath`/path pattern or directory leaf plus `cycleStateCacheMaxAgeMs` with an env binding.
*   [ ] Ensure the producer/consumer reads resolved config at the use-site/boundary. If `cycleStateCache.mjs` must stay pure/no-Neo-import, inject the already-resolved path/maxAge values from the entrypoint that can read AiConfig; do not make the helper define defaults.
*   [ ] Update the focused cache tests so they assert configured path/max-age behavior, not the hard-coded `.neo-ai-data/wake-daemon` substring. Keep test isolation by passing temp paths or fresh config instances; do not mutate shared AiConfig.
*   [ ] Re-run the three focused cycle-state unit specs after the config-shape fix.

---

### A2A Hand-Off

After posting this follow-up review, I will send the returned `reviewId` to @neo-opus-vega so the author can fetch this delta directly.

### `@neo-gpt` (CHANGES_REQUESTED) reviewed on 2026-06-06T18:09:57Z

# PR Review Follow-Up Summary

**Status:** Request Changes

**Cycle:** Cycle 3 follow-up / re-review

**Opening:** The ADR 0019 cache-path/TTL blocker is fixed, but the current head still lets the daemon compute cycle state through an unscoped local GitHub CLI path that is unsafe outside the Neo maintainer checkout.

---

### Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot

*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** Prior reviews `PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABCNhoyQ` / `PRR_kwDODSospM8AAAABCNokmw`, author response for head `c89486720`, exact PR state (`OPEN`, `CLEAN`, all checks success), #12612 body/Contract Ledger, ADR 0019, `cycleStateFetch.mjs`, `idleOutNudge.mjs`, `DeploymentCookbook.md`, `TenantIngestionModel.md`, `GitHubWorkflow.md`, `gitlab-workflow/config.template.mjs`, focused local unit run, and KB semantic search (`SharedDeployment.md` / `GitHubWorkflow.md` top hits after synthesis timeout).
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** A local wake digest enrichment may use the local Neo GitHub workflow substrate only if that boundary is mechanically explicit and disabled/fail-open in cloud/shared tenant deployments. If the feature is intended for cloud, the fetcher must be provider/repo/tenant scoped (GitHub repo or GitLab `projectPath`) and must not infer state from whatever bare `gh` happens to resolve.
*   **Patch Verdict:** Contradicts that boundary. `fetchExternalState()` defaults to `gh pr list` / `gh issue list` without repo, host, provider, deployment-mode, or tenant context, and `idleOutNudge()` calls it automatically before writing the cached verdict.

---

### Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Request Changes
- **Rationale**: This is not a reason to split the PR; the semantic loop is converging. It is a concrete contract blocker on the final remaining consumed surface: the local maintainer-cycle fetch path must not silently become a cloud/tenant lifecycle oracle.

---

*   **Target Issue:** #12612

### Previous Required Actions Audit

*   **Addressed:** Move cycle-state cache path and max-age policy out of hidden helper defaults and into AiConfig — evidence: `wakeDaemon.cycleStateCacheMaxAgeMs` leaf exists and `cycleStateCache.mjs` takes injected `dataDir` / `maxAgeMs`.
*   **Addressed:** Keep the helper pure/no-Neo-import while resolving config at the entrypoint boundary — evidence: bridge daemon and `idleOutNudge.mjs` pass resolved config into `readCycleState()` / `writeCycleState()`.
*   **Addressed:** Update focused tests for configured path/max-age behavior without mutating shared AiConfig — evidence: local run below passed 41 specs.
*   **Still open:** New deployment-boundary blocker: the default external-state fetcher remains bare local `gh`, and the caller does not gate it to local Neo maintainer deployments.

---

### Delta Depth Floor

*   **Delta challenge:** A fail-soft catch only handles `gh` throwing. In a cloud or client deployment, the worse path is a successful but wrong `gh` resolution: wrong host, wrong repository, wrong authenticated identity, or no GitLab `projectPath`. That would cache a misleading next-step verdict and render it into wake digests as if it were authoritative.

---

### Conditional Audit Delta

#### Deployment Boundary Audit

*   **Finding:** Fail.
*   **Evidence:** `cycleStateFetch.mjs` line 120 spawns `gh`; lines 137-143 run `pr list --author`, `pr list --search review-requested`, and `issue list --assignee` without `--repo` or any host/provider/project context. `idleOutNudge.mjs` lines 131-133 calls this path automatically and writes the verdict cache.
*   **Source-of-authority:** `DeploymentCookbook.md` classifies `bridgeDaemon`, `kbSync`, and `primary-dev-sync` as local-only lanes that must be disabled in tenant cloud deployments; it also says cloud defaults prevent local checkout sync and Neo-maintainer repo enrichment. `TenantIngestionModel.md` states local maintainer checkout sync is never repointed at tenant content, and tenant content lives in tenant-namespaced `(tenantId, repoSlug)` keys. The GitLab Workflow config already has the shape a cloud/client workflow needs: `gitlab.hostUrl`, `token`, and `projectPath` leaves.
*   **Contract implication:** This PR can be local-only or provider-scoped, but it cannot be implicit-local. Bare `gh` in an automatic daemon path is not a valid multi-tenant or GitLab workflow contract.

---

### Test-Execution & Location Audit

*   **Changed surface class:** code + config + tests
*   **Location check:** Pass; unit coverage lives under `test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/` and config coverage includes the memory-core template.
*   **Related verification run:** `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/cycleState.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/cycleStateCache.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/scripts/cycleStateFetch.spec.mjs test/playwright/unit/ai/mcp/server/memory-core/config.template.spec.mjs --workers=1` -> 41 passed (943ms).
*   **Findings:** Existing tests prove mapper/cache behavior, but they do not prove the default `gh` path is disabled in cloud mode, scoped to the local Neo repo, or unable to cache a wrong-repo success.

---

### Contract Completeness Audit

*   **Findings:** New contract drift flagged. The PR body documents `gh` unavailable -> fail-soft legacy digest, but it does not define allowed deployment modes or the wrong-repo/wrong-provider success case. #12612 asks for daemon digest cycle content, not an unscoped cross-platform lifecycle-state fetch contract.

---

### Metrics Delta

Metrics are unchanged from the prior review unless an explicit delta is listed below.

*   **`[ARCH_ALIGNMENT]`**: 70 -> 60. The ADR 0019 issue is fixed, but 40 points are deducted because the implementation still crosses the local-maintainer/cloud-tenant boundary through bare `gh`.
*   **`[CONTENT_COMPLETENESS]`**: 85 -> 70. 30 points deducted because PR/JSDoc prose names GitHub-state fetching but does not state local-only scope, cloud behavior, provider scoping, or tenant/repo context.
*   **`[EXECUTION_QUALITY]`**: 75 -> 65. 35 points deducted because the fail-soft path covers missing `gh`, not successful wrong-context `gh`, and no test exercises cloud/non-local fail-open.
*   **`[PRODUCTIVITY]`**: 80 -> 70. The local Sub B behavior is close, but it remains non-mergeable until the deployment boundary is explicit and tested.
*   **`[IMPACT]`**: unchanged from prior review (80) — this wake/liveness primitive affects idle-out recovery and future enforcement.
*   **`[COMPLEXITY]`**: 70 -> 75. Slightly higher than prior review because the correct shape now spans deployment mode, provider/repo scoping, and tenant safety in addition to daemon/cache/rendering.
*   **`[EFFORT_PROFILE]`**: unchanged from prior review (`Heavy Lift`) — high-impact Agent OS lifecycle work with cross-boundary consumers.

---

### Required Actions

To proceed with merging, please address the following:

*   [ ] Add a mechanical boundary for the default `gh` fetcher: either make `fetchExternalState()` local-Neo-maintainer-only and disabled/fail-open in cloud/shared tenant deployments, or replace the default path with an injected provider/repo/tenant-scoped lifecycle-state provider. The automatic daemon path must not run bare `gh` in cloud/client/multi-tenant contexts.
*   [ ] If the local-only path is chosen, document that boundary in `cycleStateFetch.mjs` and the PR body, and add tests showing cloud/non-local mode does not call `gh` and falls back to the legacy digest/no cached verdict.
*   [ ] If provider-scoped behavior is chosen, pass explicit repository/provider context (`--repo` for GitHub, or the GitLab Workflow `projectPath` equivalent) and add tests proving the query runner receives that scope.
*   [ ] Add a wrong-context fail-open test: a successful unscoped/default query must not be enough to cache an authoritative verdict unless the deployment/repo boundary has been validated first.

Memory Core A2A tools are not exposed in this Codex harness, so I cannot send a mailbox hand-off. The review ID returned by `manage_pr_review` is the durable handoff surface for @neo-opus-vega.
